channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: UK police probe possible Iran link after Jewish charity ambulances set on fire
item.description: Police in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic hate crime attack after four vehicles belonging to a Jewish ambulance service were set on fire.
item.content:encoded:
Police in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic hate crime attack after four vehicles belonging to a Jewish ambulance service were set on fire.

(Image credit: Alberto Pezzali)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Despite state bans and restrictions, the number of abortions in the U.S. holds steady
item.description: The Guttmacher Institute has a new analysis on how many abortions happened in 2025.
item.content:encoded:
The Guttmacher Institute has a new analysis on how many abortions happened in 2025.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Markwayne Mullin confirmed as the next secretary of Homeland Security
item.description: The Oklahoma Republican comes to the helm in the midst of a shutdown that has left some 100,000 of the department's more than a quarter-million employees working without pay.
item.content:encoded:
The Oklahoma Republican comes to the helm in the midst of a shutdown that has left some 100,000 of the department's more than a quarter-million employees working without pay.

(Image credit: Manuel Balce Ceneta)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Trump administration places Christopher Columbus statue on White House grounds
item.description: The Trump administration placed a statue of Christopher Columbus on the White House grounds. Some people are not happy about it.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration placed a statue of Christopher Columbus on the White House grounds. Some people are not happy about it.

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Trump administration to pay French company $1B to drop U.S. offshore wind leases
item.description: TotalEnergies has agreed to what's essentially a refund of its leases for projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York, and will invest the money in fossil fuel projects instead, the Department of Interior announced.
item.content:encoded:
TotalEnergies has agreed to what's essentially a refund of its leases for projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York, and will invest the money in fossil fuel projects instead, the Department of Interior announced.

(Image credit: Thomas Padilla)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Supreme Court skeptical of laws counting mail-in ballots after election day
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments today in a case that tests whether states should be allowed to count ballots that are mailed on time but arrive after Election Day. The case could have broad implications.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court heard arguments today in a case that tests whether states should be allowed to count ballots that are mailed on time but arrive after Election Day. The case could have broad implications.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: 8 architecture and culture groups sue Trump and the Kennedy Center board
item.description: The groups, which include the American Institute of Architects, are asking for compliance with historic preservation laws and to secure approval from Congress.
item.content:encoded:
The groups, which include the American Institute of Architects, are asking for compliance with historic preservation laws and to secure approval from Congress.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Riz Ahmed is his own worst critic. His new show 'Bait' explores that
item.description: In his Prime Video series, Ahmed plays a struggling actor auditioning to be the next James Bond. Ahmed says Bond is a "symbol of aspiration, this unattainable kind of self" his character is pursuing.
item.content:encoded:
In his Prime Video series, Ahmed plays a struggling actor auditioning to be the next James Bond. Ahmed says Bond is a "symbol of aspiration, this unattainable kind of self" his character is pursuing.

(Image credit: Tristan Fewings)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Supreme Court declines to review press freedom case
item.description: At issue was the 2017 arrest in Texas of a journalist who published news stories about a border agent's public suicide and a car crash.
item.content:encoded:
At issue was the 2017 arrest in Texas of a journalist who published news stories about a border agent's public suicide and a car crash.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Kapasi

item.title: Voice of America staffers sue, alleging Kari Lake put on propaganda
item.description: Voice of America staffers are suing Trump administration official Kari Lake, alleging she put pro-Trump propaganda on its airwaves. She has lost numerous rulings of late.
item.content:encoded:
Voice of America staffers are suing Trump administration official Kari Lake, alleging she put pro-Trump propaganda on its airwaves. She has lost numerous rulings of late.

(Image credit: Voice of America)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik
